svg.text: Lick-Wilmerding High School 4-Year Course Selection Guide
Automatic Enrollment or No Action Required
Required History, English, Math, Science, or Langauge Class Choice
Required Flex or Free Elective Choice
Optional Flex or Free Elective Choice

g.text: Your Flex Elective Pool
Complete these 5 flex electives with flexible timing: complete Performing Arts any year (9th–12th) and all other items in 10th–12th grade only.
☐ Performing Arts
1 semester · 9th–12th grade
☐ Technical Arts: Design & Engineering
1 semester · 10th–12th grade
☐ Technical Arts: Design & Craft
1 semester · 10th–12th grade
☐ G Elective (1st of 2)
1 semester · 10th–12th grade
☐ G Elective (2nd of 2)
1 semester · 10th–12th grade
→ At every open elective slot: use it for an unchecked pool item first. All 5 checked? Use it as a free elective. Rank ≥5 alternates.
9th Grade
Course request window opens in May via Admissions — not the April window
Auto-enrolled — no choices needed (all year-long):
Body-Mind Education · Ethnic Studies · Biology · English 1 · Design & Technology · Contemporary Media & Art
Satisfies: Ethnic Studies + Body-Mind Education req, Science yr 1, English yr 1, Technical Arts 9th req, Fine Art (CMA) req
Required: take placement tests to determine your Math and World Language courses
Your scores place you into the appropriate starting level for both subjects.
Contact the Admissions Office for placement test details and scheduling.
Want a Performing Arts class this year?
(optional — adds an 8th class one semester)
Yes
Request your first-choice Performing Arts course.
Rank ≥5 other Performing Arts options as alternates.
✓ Check off Performing Arts
in your Flex Pool.
⚠ Audition/permission required:
Chamber Orch + Jazz/Contemporary Ensemble
Contact Jason Gillenwater by Apr 23.
Rank alternates in your order of preference.
The 8th class is not guaranteed — strong alternates
help ensure you get the Performing Arts course you want.
No
No action needed.
Performing Arts stays in your Flex Pool
— available any time 10th–12th.
10th Grade
4 course selection requests per semester
Auto-enrolled (all year-long):
Chemistry · English 2 · World History
None of these use your 4 selection slots.
Required year-long — uses 2 slots per semester
1. Math — request the next course in your progression track
2. World Languages — request the next course in your progression track
Changing tracks or planning summer coursework?
Contact José Perez (Math) or Ms. Larson (World Languages) before Apr 23.
Semester with Health
Slot 3 (required): Sophomore Health
Request for this semester.
✓ Completes your Body-Mind Education requirement.
Slot 4 (optional 7th class):
Flex Pool item, free elective, Teaching Assistant, or independent study.
Teaching Assistant / Independent Study: have an informal conversation with the teacher first.
Note their name and class using the notepad icon when requesting.
Official Teaching Assistant / Independent Study form releases in early August — complete it then.
Haven't decided? You have until end of week 2 of the semester.
Skip this slot if you don't want a 7th class.
Semester without Health
Slot 3 (required): Elective (class minimum = 6):
Flex Pool item or free elective. Rank ≥5 alternates.
Performing / Technical / Visual Arts or G elective.
Slot 4 (optional 7th class):
Flex Pool item, free elective, Teaching Assistant, or independent study.
Teaching Assistant / Independent Study: have an informal conversation with the teacher first.
Note their name and class using the notepad icon when requesting.
Official Teaching Assistant / Independent Study form releases in early August — complete it then.
Haven't decided? You have until end of week 2 of the semester.
Skip this slot if you don't want a 7th class.
11th Grade
5 course selection requests per semester
Auto-enrolled (both year-long):
English 3 Honors · US History Honors
Neither uses a selection slot.
Required year-long — uses 1 slot per semester
2. Science elective — request your first-choice course.
Then rank all other science courses as alternates (not just 5 — rank all of them).
⚠ Skipping any from the list means losing scheduling priority.
Ethics in the Sciences counts as a G elective, not a lab science — exclude it here.
Honors Physics prereq issue? Contact Paul McCullough.
Math and World Languages — check which apply (year-long, 1 slot each per semester)
☐ Math 3-year req not yet met → request next level. Track change? Contact José Perez before Apr 23.
☐ World Languages 3-year + Level 3 not yet met → request next level. Track change? Contact Ms. Larson before Apr 23.
✓ Math already complete → that slot is free: use for Flex Pool or free elective.
✓ World Languages already complete → same: redirect that slot to Flex Pool or free elective.
6th class (required to meet six class minimum):
Flex Pool item first, or free elective (Performing / Technical / Visual Arts or G elective). Rank ≥5 alternates.
7th class (optional, not guaranteed):
Flex Pool item, free elective, Teaching Assistant, or independent study.
Teaching Assistant / Independent Study: have an informal conversation with the teacher first.
Note their name and class using the notepad icon when requesting.
Official Teaching Assistant / Independent Study form releases in early August — complete it then.
Haven't decided? You have until end of week 2 of the semester.
Skip this slot entirely if you don't want a 7th class.
12th Grade
7 course selection requests per semester
Before you start: tally all outstanding requirements
Check: Math done (3 years)? · World Languages done (3 years + Level 3)? · Science elective done? · All 5 Flex Pool items checked?
Every incomplete item below takes required slots. With 7 slots per semester, there is plenty of room — but count carefully.
Required every semester — 1 slot
1. Honors English 4 — request one per semester. Rank all Honors English 4 options as alternates.
⚠ Creative Writing and Journalism do not count as senior English — exclude them from this list.
✓ Completes your English 4-year requirement.
Required every semester — 1 slot
2. History Seminar — one per semester (Fall seminar for Fall, Spring seminar for Spring).
Rank all History Seminars as alternates.
✓ Completes your History 3-year requirement.
Any core requirements still outstanding? Each takes 1 slot per semester (year-long).
☐ Math not complete → request next level. Track change? Contact José Perez before Apr 23.
☐ World Languages not complete → request next level. Track change? Contact Ms. Larson before Apr 23.
☐ Science elective not done → request course. Rank all science courses as alternates.
Losing any from the alternates list means losing scheduling priority.
Ethics in the Sciences counts as G elective only — exclude it from science alternates.
Complete your Flex Pool — this is your last chance.
Any unchecked items must be finished this year. Each one takes a required slot.
Technical Arts check: your Technical Arts electives across 10th–12th must include
☐ one Design & Engineering course AND one Design & Craft course.
These are separate categories — verify both are covered before finalizing.
Rank ≥5 alternates for each elective.
6th class (required to meet class minimum):
Must come from Performing / Technical / Visual Arts or a G-designated elective.
Rank ≥5 alternates.
7th class (optional, not guaranteed):
Must come from Performing Arts / Technical Arts / Visual Arts / G elective,
or Teaching Assistant / independent study.
Teaching Assistant / Independent Study: talk informally with the teacher first.
Note their name and class using the notepad icon when requesting.
Official form releases in early August — complete it with your teacher then.
Haven't decided? You have until end of week 2 of the semester.
Skip this slot if you don't want a 7th class.
Doubling opportunity (optional)
If your World Language, Math, and Science
requirements are all complete, you may take
a 2nd A–E course (a=History, b=English, c=Math,
d=Lab Science, e=World Languages), for 5 total A–E classes.
Rank ≥5 alternates.
Add/Drop
Schedules released August 3 · Add/drop window: August 5 (8am) – August 12 (11:59pm)
Spring add/drop window in November (semester courses only)
Year-long courses cannot be dropped or section-switched after the fall window closes (Dean approval only, extremely rare).
Year-long course conflicting with a spring elective? Switch your year-long section during the fall window.
Submit a Scheduling Error Form immediately (before add/drop opens) if any of these apply:
Missing a graduation requirement · Wrong class count · Missing a prerequisite · Enrolled in a class you already took
Sophomore not enrolled in Health · Senior missing English or History any semester · Under- or over-enrolled
Class count: 9th grade = 7 classes (8 max) · 10th–12th = 6 classes (7 max)
Course request window: April 12, 5pm – April 23, 11:59pm · Cannot edit or submit after deadline
Click Save before exiting course requests.
Jeff Treviño, PhD — April 2026
